title: Left and Right Sum Differences
performanceTarget: top 1% execution time and overall performance
note: Pareto-optimal challenge: the single recommended solution is also the speed extreme and the memory extreme. Per the repo standard (no coincident files), only solution.cpp is shipped; there is no separate solution-runtime or solution-memory file.

variants.languageReason: The problem is a tiny arithmetic-only linear scan over contiguous arrays. Native C++ avoids interpreter and GC overhead, uses vector<int> directly in the LeetCode signature, and keeps the required output allocation compact.
variants.approachJustification: Compute the total sum once, then scan left to right. Subtract the current value from the remaining right sum, write abs(left - right), and add the current value to the left sum. This also serves as the speed and memory extreme.

reasoningSummary: The total sum lets rightSum[i] be maintained as the remaining total after removing nums[i], while a running left sum tracks all prior elements. This avoids the hinted leftSum/rightSum arrays and reaches the linear output lower bound with constant auxiliary memory.
